Field.Label:
All sensitive values (API keys, bearer tokens, usernames, passwords, private host addresses) are read from the master IndigoSecrets.py at /Library/Application Support/Perceptive Automation/IndigoSecrets.py FIRST. The fields below are a fallback only — leave them blank if you have the matching key in IndigoSecrets.py. If a value is set in NEITHER place, the plugin logs an ERROR pointing here and skips that feature.
Keys this plugin reads: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY - Anthropic Claude API key (required) CLAUDEBRIDGE_BEARER_TOKEN - IWS bearer token for the MCP proxy INFLUXDB_HOST / PORT / USERNAME / PASSWORD / DATABASE (optional)

Field.tooltip: OPTIONAL — only needed for AI summaries in the historical-analysis tool. All other tools work without it.
Field.Label: Anthropic API Key (optional):

Optional — every MCP tool works without a key here (Claude Code uses your own Claude account). Only the AI summaries in the historical-analysis tool need one. If you want those, get a key from https://console.anthropic.com/settings/keys, or set ANTHROPIC_API_KEY in /Library/Application Support/Perceptive Automation/IndigoSecrets.py and leave this field blank.
Enable InfluxDB Historical Data:
Field.Description: Enable access to historical device data stored in InfluxDB

Field.Label: Leave the fields below blank if INFLUXDB_HOST/PORT/USERNAME/PASSWORD/DATABASE are set in IndigoSecrets.py — those take priority.
InfluxDB Host:
Field.Description: InfluxDB host (e.g. 192.168.x.x) — the IndigoSecrets.py key for this is INFLUXDB_HOST. Protocol prefix is stripped.

Field.Label: InfluxDB Port:
Field.Description: InfluxDB server port (default: 8086) — the IndigoSecrets.py key for this is INFLUXDB_PORT.

Field.Label: InfluxDB Database Name:
Field.Description: Name of the database containing historical data (default: indigo)

Field.Label: Read-cache TTL (seconds):
Field.Description: How long read-only tool results stay cached. 0 disables cache. Max 300.

Field.Label: Mutating tools (turn_on/off, fire_indigo_event, …) automatically invalidate related cache buckets. Clients can override with the Cache-Control: no-cache header.

Per-token scopes (read / write / admin) are configured in scopes.json under the plugin's Preferences folder. Without scopes.json, every authenticated token gets full access (backward compatible). See Show Plugin Info menu for the path.

Event Webhooks let the home POST a signed event to an external URL when a device/variable condition is met (the "home calls out" feature). OFF by default. SECURITY: targets are default-deny — only hosts on the allow-list below (or in IndigoSecrets.py WEBHOOK_ALLOWLIST, read first) can be registered. Private/LAN ranges need an explicit CIDR (e.g. 192.168.100.50/32). The 3 webhook tools are ADMIN-scope.
Egress allow-list:
Field.Description: Comma/newline-separated hosts, IPs or CIDRs the home may POST to. Forms: hooks.example.com, *.example.com, 203.0.113.5, 192.168.100.50/32. Blank = deny all. Unioned with IndigoSecrets.py WEBHOOK_ALLOWLIST.

Field.Label: Plain-HTTP allow-list:
Field.Description: Hosts/IPs permitted over plain http (no TLS) — for LAN receivers only. https is the default and is always allowed for allow-listed hosts.

Field.Label: Click to test Anthropic API and InfluxDB connections with current configuration
Auto-configure Claude Code:
Field.Description: When enabled (default), the plugin copies indigo_mcp_proxy.py to /Library/Application Support/Perceptive Automation/Scripts/ on startup, patches the Bearer token, and edits ~/.mcp.json and ~/.claude/settings.json so Claude Code can connect without manual setup. Disable if you manage these files yourself.
